claims.text: Slow institutional decline, not one great fire (= Bagnall's argument)
claims.snippet: “a whole literature of wishful thinking has grown up” about its destruction

claims.text: Ptolemy VIII expelled foreign scholars ~145 BCE
claims.snippet: “As early as 145 BCE… expelled all foreign scholars”

claims.text: Decline came from lost patronage/funding
claims.snippet: “a loss of patronage… leading to a decline in upkeep”

claims.text: Did NOT invent movable type (China ~1040; Korea by 1377)
claims.snippet: “invented around 1040 CE… by Bi Sheng”

claims.text: ~30,000 editions / ~20M copies in Europe by 1500
claims.snippet: “By 1500 the presses of Western Europe had produced more than twenty million copies.” (~30,000 known editions)

claims.text: Malleus Maleficarum: 28 editions 1486–1600
claims.snippet: “28 editions between 1486 and 1600”

claims.text: Real wages did NOT rise immediately — nominal up, inflation ate it, real wages lagged to later 1370s; immediate gain = leverage
claims.snippet: “cash wages rose, [but] inflation reduced the purchasing power”

claims.text: ~1347–1351; a third to half of Europe died (Europe-wide)
claims.snippet: “between 30 and 60%… died.” Your 1/8–2/3 is the regional variation range.

claims.text: Statute of Labourers 1351 froze wages at 1346 levels; fed the 1381 Revolt
claims.snippet: “maximum wage… commensurate with… 1346”; a cause of the Peasants' Revolt

claims.text: Bubble was real but NOT an economy-wide catastrophe (Goldgar) — entry debunks the myth
claims.snippet: “couldn't find a single bankrupt… what suffered was trust”

claims.text: Mackay: “Men… think in herds… go mad in herds… recover their senses slowly, and one by one” (verbatim)
claims.snippet: matches Project Gutenberg text exactly

claims.text: Nixon: “…suspend temporarily the convertibility of the dollar into gold…” (verbatim)
claims.snippet: matches the Aug 15 1971 address transcript

claims.text: Aug 15 1971 ended dollar–gold convertibility; floating by 1973
claims.snippet: “Nixon announced the US would end… convertibility”

claims.text: B-59 / Arkhipov: nuclear torpedo, 3-officer rule, Arkhipov refused
claims.snippet: “On most Soviet submarines… only the captain and political officer were required to authorize a launch. On B-59, a third signature was needed because Vasili Arkhipov was also chief of staff of the brigade.”

claims.text: Secret Jupiter-Turkey deal (RFK–Dobrynin), kept quiet
claims.snippet: confirmed incl. the secrecy motive

claims.text: Fleming Nobel lecture: the resistance warning (verbatim)
claims.snippet: matches nobelprize.org lecture PDF, Dec 11 1945

claims.text: Fleming 1928; Oxford team 1940 mouse experiment; 1945 Nobel
claims.snippet: ACS landmark + Nobel summary

claims.text: June 1215 Runnymede; sealed not signed; preempts the “democracy” myth
claims.snippet: The National Archives (UK) resource

claims.text: Clause 39 (“No free man shall be seized…”) — faithful translation
claims.snippet: Magna Carta Project (UEA), Clause 39

claims.text: Annulled by Pope Innocent III within ~10 weeks (24 Aug 1215)
claims.snippet: papal bull declared it “null and void… for ever”

claims.text: 730 delegates / 44 nations / July 1944; IMF + World Bank

claims.text: “Built to survive nuclear war” is a MYTH — entry debunks it correctly
